cases.passage: Evans, an IT specialist, had been applying for a job as a network administrator for months. Despite having excellent qualifications and experience, he had not received any positive responses from potential employers. Evans was frustrated and felt that his skills and qualifications were not being recognized. He decided to take an O-level computer studies course to improve his chances of getting hired. The course helped him gain a deeper understanding of computer systems and networking. Evans also learned how to write effective CVs and prepare for job interviews. With his newfound skills and confidence, Evans was able to secure a job as a network administrator at a reputable company.

questions.q: Why did Evans decide to take an O-level computer studies course?
opts: (A) To improve his knowledge of computer systems and networking
(B) To gain confidence in his skills and qualifications
(C) To learn how to write effective CVs and prepare for job interviews
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Evans decided to take the course for all the above reasons, which ultimately helped him secure a job as a network administrator.

questions.q: What was Evans' initial frustration due to?
opts: (A) Lack of experience
(B) Lack of qualifications
(C) Lack of recognition of his skills and qualifications
(D) Lack of job opportunities
questions.explanation: Evans felt that his skills and qualifications were not being recognized, which led to his frustration.

questions.q: What did Evans learn from the O-level computer studies course?
opts: (A) How to write effective CVs and prepare for job interviews
(B) How to gain confidence in his skills and qualifications
(C) How to improve his knowledge of computer systems and networking
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Evans learned all the above skills and knowledge from the course, which helped him secure a job as a network administrator.

questions.q: What was the outcome of Evans taking the O-level computer studies course?
opts: (A) He lost his job as an IT specialist
(B) He gained confidence in his skills and qualifications
(C) He secured a job as a network administrator
(D) He improved his knowledge of computer systems and networking
questions.explanation: With his newfound skills and confidence, Evans was able to secure a job as a network administrator at a reputable company.

questions.q: Why was Evans' decision to take the O-level computer studies course a good one?
opts: (A) He gained confidence in his skills and qualifications
(B) He improved his knowledge of computer systems and networking
(C) He learned how to write effective CVs and prepare for job interviews
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Evans' decision to take the course was a good one because he gained confidence in his skills and qualifications, improved his knowledge of computer systems and networking, and learned how to write effective CVs and prepare for job interviews.

cases.passage: Evans had always been interested in computers and technology. After completing his O-level computer studies course, he decided to pursue a career in IT. He applied for various jobs, but most of them required a degree in computer science. Evans was frustrated again, feeling that his O-level qualification was not recognized by potential employers. He decided to take a gap year to gain more experience and build a strong portfolio. During this time, he worked on various personal projects, including developing a mobile app and creating a website. Evans also volunteered to help his friends and family with their computer-related problems. With his newfound experience and skills, Evans was able to secure a job as a junior IT specialist.

questions.q: Why was Evans frustrated after completing his O-level computer studies course?
opts: (A) He found the course too difficult
(B) He felt that his O-level qualification was not recognized by potential employers
(C) He did not gain any experience or skills
(D) He did not apply for any jobs
questions.explanation: Evans felt that his O-level qualification was not recognized by potential employers, which led to his frustration.

questions.q: What did Evans do during his gap year?
opts: (A) He worked on various personal projects
(B) He volunteered to help his friends and family with their computer-related problems
(C) He applied for various jobs
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Evans worked on various personal projects, volunteered to help his friends and family, and applied for various jobs during his gap year.

questions.q: What skills and experience did Evans gain during his gap year?
opts: (A) How to develop a mobile app and create a website
(B) How to help friends and family with computer-related problems
(C) How to apply for jobs and build a strong portfolio
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Evans gained all the above skills and experience during his gap year, which helped him secure a job as a junior IT specialist.

questions.q: What was the outcome of Evans taking a gap year?
opts: (A) He lost his interest in computers and technology
(B) He gained experience and skills, but not a job
(C) He secured a job as a junior IT specialist
(D) He improved his knowledge of computer systems and networking
questions.explanation: With his newfound experience and skills, Evans was able to secure a job as a junior IT specialist.

questions.q: Why was Evans' decision to take a gap year a good one?
opts: (A) He gained experience and skills
(B) He secured a job as a junior IT specialist
(C) He improved his knowledge of computer systems and networking
(D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Evans' decision to take a gap year was a good one because he gained experience and skills, secured a job as a junior IT specialist, and improved his knowledge of computer systems and networking.
